metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile 'What' and 'Call-site (run 3)': strike the `create_prompt` call from the `author_criterion_overlay` description in 'What', or add an explicit note that Run 3 supersedes it, so there is one unambiguous owner of the `create_prompt` invocation.
findings.finding: The 'What' section specifies that `criterion_preview.author_criterion_overlay` writes the rubric via `create_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(cid), ...)`, but the 'Call-site responsibility (run 3)' section contradicts this by naming `editor._library_create` as the ONLY site that calls `create_prompt` with `criterion_prompt_id`, and says `author_criterion_overlay` is 'RENAMED/refocused' to NOT call `create_prompt`. An implementer reading only 'What' would place the `create_prompt` call inside `author_criterion_overlay`; one reading Run 3 would place it in `_library_create`. These are architecturally incompatible.
findings.location: 'What' section (editor authoring bullet) vs 'Call-site responsibility (run 3)' section (items 2 and 3)
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, replace the `author_criterion_overlay` bullet's mention of `create_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(cid), ...)` with a note that the prompt write is delegated to the orchestrator (`_library_create`), and that `author_criterion_overlay` handles only the overlay routing + activate write.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and update `src/rebar/llm/eval.py:410` (and `eval_solver.py:72` + `production_batch_runner.py:177-178`) to route through `criterion_prompt_id` when constructing the prompt id from a `project.<name>` criterion id, since these sites currently use the raw `f'plan-review-{criterion_id}'` pattern that will yield a dotted, `_valid_id`-invalid string for project criteria.
findings.finding: `eval.py:410` constructs `f'plan-review-{criterion_id}'` for any criterion id, including a `project.<name>` criterion. After this change, a `project.foo` criterion would produce the prompt id `plan-review-project.foo` — a dotted, `_valid_id`-invalid string — when accessed via the eval path. The plan's Scope section does not list `src/rebar/llm/eval.py` as a file to update, and the Acceptance Criteria don't cover the eval call-site. This is a real breakage scenario, not a theoretical one.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval.py` to the Scope file list and update the `prompt_id` derivation at line 410 to use `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` (imported from `criteria.ids`) instead of `f'plan-review-{criterion_id}'`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's description: the `plan-review-` strip currently lives in `author_criterion_overlay` (criterion_preview.py:535), NOT in `_library_create`; the refactor must remove the strip from `author_criterion_overlay` (not from `_library_create`) when the function is renamed to accept a dotted `criterion_id` directly.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Call-site responsibility (run 3)' section states that `_library_create` 'today strips `plan-review-` off the prompt id to recover `cid`'. Reading the actual code at `editor.py:740`, `_library_create` passes `pid` (the raw POST body id) directly to `author_criterion_overlay(str(_repo_root), pid, routing)` — it does NOT strip anything. The stripping happens inside `author_criterion_overlay` at `criterion_preview.py:535`. This means the plan's proposed refactor in run 3 — where `_library_create` itself becomes the ONE site that applies `criterion_prompt_id` before `create_prompt` — is correctly targeted, but the motivating description of the current code is factually wrong (the stripping is NOT in `_library_create`). An implementer reading the plan would need to correct their model of where the current id-recovery happens.
findings.suggested_fix: In 'Call-site responsibility (run 3)', item 2, replace '(Today `_library_create` strips `plan-review-` off the prompt id to recover `cid`)' with '(Today `author_criterion_overlay` strips `plan-review-` off the prompt id at `criterion_preview.py:535` to recover `cid` — this strip must be removed when the function is refactored to accept a dotted `criterion_id` directly)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the new validation in `_validate_routing_entry` (or the overlay merge loop in `_effective_routing_cached`) will explicitly reject a `project.<name>` criterion id where `<name>` contains a `.` (e.g. `project.foo.bar`) or fails `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*`, since no such check currently exists in `overlay.py` and the plan's injectivity guarantee depends on it.
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' section and Scope both assert that `_validate_routing_entry` (or 'the namespace check') is where the dot-free project-name charset constraint will be enforced. Reading `overlay.py:130-198`, `_validate_routing_entry` validates `exec`, `block_threshold`, `default_posture`, `fail_mode`, `disabled`, and `applies_at` — but contains NO validation of the criterion id FORMAT (e.g. it does not check that a `project.foo.bar` id has only one dot, or that the name part is `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*`). The plan states this check 'validate this in `_validate_routing_entry` / the namespace check' but there is currently no namespace-name charset validation there. This makes the plan's stated injectivity guarantee ('a project name is constrained to a DOT-FREE charset') entirely dependent on a new validation that has no existing hook point confirmed by the code.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/eval.py` (`calibrate_criterion`, line 410) to the edit scope: update the prompt-id construction to use `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` from `criteria.ids`, and update `test_criteria_eval.py`'s assertion on `r['prompt']` to match the sanitized form.
findings.finding: `eval.py:calibrate_criterion` (line 410) constructs the prompt id as `f'plan-review-{criterion_id}'`, producing `plan-review-project.test` for `criterion_id='project.test'`. After this plan's change the sanitized prompt file is `plan-review-project-test.md`, so `calibrate_criterion` will fail to load the rubric for any project criterion. This consumer is outside `src/rebar/llm/criteria|plan_review|workflow` and is NOT listed in the plan's scope.
findings.suggested_fix: In eval.py line 409-410, replace the inline `f'plan-review-{criterion_id}'` with `from rebar.llm.criteria.ids import criterion_prompt_id; prompt_id = criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)`. Update test_criteria_eval.py:72 to assert `r['prompt'] == 'plan-review-project-test'`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_solver.py` to the edit scope: `_criterion_id` must reverse-map a sanitized prompt id (`plan-review-project-foo`) back to the dotted criterion id (`project.foo`), either by carrying the dotted id explicitly or by applying the inverse mapping for the `project-` namespace prefix.
findings.finding: `eval_solver.py:_criterion_id` (line 72) strips the `plan-review-` prefix to recover the registry criterion id: `cid = prompt_id[len('plan-review-'):]`. After the change, a project criterion's prompt id is `plan-review-project-foo`; stripping the prefix yields `project-foo`, not `project.foo`, so `registry.by_id().get('project-foo')` returns `None` and the criterion is silently treated as a non-criterion. This file is not listed in the plan's scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `CLAUDE.md` and `docs/adr/0015-project-supplied-criteria.md` to the edit scope to update the documented prompt-file path from `plan-review-project.<name>.md` to `plan-review-project-<name>.md` (dot→dash).
findings.finding: `CLAUDE.md` (line 268) and `docs/adr/0015-project-supplied-criteria.md` (line 61) both explicitly document the OLD dotted prompt-path form `plan-review-project.<name>.md`. After this change the canonical form is `plan-review-project-<name>.md`. These documentation consumers are unenumerated in the plan's scope.
findings.suggested_fix: In CLAUDE.md line 268 change `plan-review-project.<name>.md` to `plan-review-project-<name>.md`. In ADR 0015 line 61 make the same substitution.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define `criterion_prompt_id`'s full error contract in the plan: specify the exception type it raises (e.g. `CriteriaError`/`InvalidPromptIdError`) and exact message for each invalid-input case (`project.` with empty name, `project.foo.bar` with extra dot, a built-in id passed with a dot), so both authoring and resolution sites handle it consistently.
findings.finding: `criterion_prompt_id` is referenced throughout the plan as the single canonical mapping function but does not exist anywhere in the codebase (`src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` is absent); its error contract for invalid inputs (e.g. a doubly-dotted name `project.foo.bar` or an empty name `project.`) is never defined.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the `ids.py` spec: 'raises `CriteriaError` with a located message if the input has more than one `.` or if the name part (after `project.`) is empty or contains characters outside `[A-Za-z0-9-]`; a built-in id (no `.`) is always returned as-is.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or add to the plan) that the descriptor-resolution `_get_prompt` closure in `_descriptor_from_prompt` (registry.py:233-236) is updated in the same changeset as the authoring path — there is no fallback specified if these diverge, and a partial update breaks `load_criteria` for the whole repo.
findings.finding: The descriptor-resolution site (`_descriptor_from_prompt` / `registry._get_prompt` at registry.py:233-236) currently constructs the prompt id as `f"plan-review-{criterion_id}"` verbatim; for a project criterion `project.foo` this forms the dotted string `plan-review-project.foo` which (a) fails `_valid_id` and (b) no such file exists. The plan names this as a required change but specifies no fallback for the window between when a project criterion is activated in the overlay and when `_descriptor_from_prompt` is updated to use `criterion_prompt_id`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the plan (and implementation spec) the exact location and regex for the project-name charset check inside `_validate_routing_entry` — specifically that it runs after the `_PROJECT_PREFIX` check and enforces `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*` on the `<name>` part of any `project.<name>` id.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that `_validate_routing_entry` in `overlay.py` must be updated to enforce a dot-free charset on the project name (to make `criterion_prompt_id` injective), but the existing function (overlay.py:130-198) has NO such check, and the plan does not specify where in the function body this check is inserted or what the exact regex/rule is relative to the existing `_PROJECT_PREFIX` check.
findings.suggested_fix: After the `cid.startswith(_PROJECT_PREFIX)` check in `_validate_routing_entry`, extract `name = cid[len(_PROJECT_PREFIX):]` and validate `bool(re.fullmatch(r'[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*', name))`; raise `CriteriaError` with a message citing the located overlay file and the offending id.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add AC/test cases covering the failure path of the new validation constraint: verify that `project.a.b`, `project.a_b`, and other non-conforming names are rejected with a located error (not silently accepted or mapped).
findings.finding: No failure-path or invalid-input tests are specified for the new validation constraint: a `project.<name>` with a dotted/underscored name is described as 'a located error' but no AC or test requirement covers this rejection path, nor the charset enforcement boundary.
findings.location: Scope section ('Project-name charset (RESOLVED)') and AC line 3 ('a name with a `.` is a located error')
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'Attempting to author a criterion with id `project.a.b` or `project.a_b` returns a located validation error (400) and writes nothing to disk.' Add a unit test for `_validate_routing_entry` (or overlay load) that asserts rejection of each invalid name pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `criterion_prompt_id` (or its call sites) validates the project name portion is dot-free BEFORE writing the prompt file — confirm that the proposed `_validate_routing_entry` charset check, the editor form input constraint, and `criterion_prompt_id` together cover ALL entry points so a two-dot name (e.g. `project.foo.bar`) can never produce a dot-containing filename.
findings.finding: The plan's injectivity claim for `criterion_prompt_id` depends entirely on a NEW charset constraint (project name must be dot-free, i.e. `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*`) that does not currently exist in `_validate_routing_entry`. The existing validator (overlay.py:130–198) checks `exec`, `block_threshold`, `default_posture`, `fail_mode`, `disabled`, and `applies_at` — but never validates the format of the `cid` key itself. Today, an overlay entry keyed `'project.foo.bar'` (two dots) passes the current load path (it starts with `'project.'`) and would produce a dot-containing filename `plan-review-project.foo-bar.md`, breaking both the `_valid_id` invariant and the injectivity guarantee. The plan asserts this will be fixed by adding the constraint, but does not verify that the overlay-load path is the ONLY entry point — the editor POST (`/library/create`) passes `pid` directly to `create_prompt` before `author_criterion_overlay`, so a dotted-name submitted from the editor could write a dot-containing prompt file before the overlay validation fires.
findings.suggested_fix: Have `criterion_prompt_id` assert (or raise a located error) when given a `project.<name>` id where `<name>` itself contains a dot, rather than relying solely on the downstream overlay validation. This makes the function self-defending: `if '.' in name: raise ValueError(f"project name {name!r} must be dot-free")` before the mapping. Pair with a test that passes `project.foo.bar` to `criterion_prompt_id` and expects a located error.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact form UX protocol for the `project.<name>` id requirement: either (a) the id field accepts only the `<name>` part and the form auto-prepends `project.` before POST, or (b) the field accepts the full `project.<name>` and client-side validation enforces the prefix; include this in the AC for the editor form.
findings.finding: The editor authoring form's `AuthoringIdEntry` (rebarProvider.jsx:1089-1106) shows a label 'new id (letters/digits/dashes)' and accepts a free-text id with no `project.` prefix enforcement, auto-prepend, or derived display of the resulting criterion id. The plan's AC says 'the editor authoring form surfaces the resulting criterion id (`project.<name>`) so the user sees what will be created', but the exact UI protocol (auto-prepend vs. display-only label vs. separate name+prefix fields) is nowhere specified.
findings.location: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx` `AuthoringIdEntry` (lines 1089-1106) / Plan AC: 'The editor authoring form surfaces the resulting criterion id'
findings.suggested_fix: Option (a) — a read-only 'project.' prefix chip + a `<name>` text field — is the clearest UX and avoids ambiguity in the POST body construction. The `onSave` handler would assemble `id: 'project.' + nameField.trim()` before posting.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the `criterion_prompt_id` decoupling decision (rationale, mapping rule, dot-free name charset invariant) in an ADR or CLAUDE.md update so future contributors know the canonical mapping lives in `ids.py` and why.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural pattern (decoupling logical criterion id from physical rubric filename via a canonical `criterion_prompt_id` mapping) and cites an existing ADR (0015) for the namespace rule, but does not call for any ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc update to capture this new id→filename mapping decision.
findings.location: ## What — decouple the logical id from the physical filename / ## Scope
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief ADR (or append to ADR 0015 if it covers the project-criterion namespace) documenting: (1) logical id is `project.<name>`, (2) physical rubric filename is derived via `criterion_prompt_id` in `criteria/ids.py`, (3) project names are constrained to `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*` (dot-free) to keep the map injective, (4) the mapping is one-way (no reverse derivation).

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the editor's non-happy-path UX for criterion authoring: (a) client-side or server-side validation of the name charset with a human-readable error message surfaced in the form, (b) a distinct success vs. failure UI state transition, and (c) what the user sees when the overlay write fails after the prompt has been written.
findings.finding: The editor's user-facing error handling for server-side validation failures is not described. When a user submits a criterion name containing a disallowed character (`.` or `_`) or when the overlay write fails after the prompt has already been written, the plan does not specify what the editor displays — leaving the user potentially facing a raw HTTP 400/500 with no actionable guidance.
findings.location: AC: 'Project names are constrained to a dot-free charset ... a name with a `.` is a located error'; 'Call-site responsibility' step 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a client-side regex check in `rebarProvider.jsx` on the criterion name field (same `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*` rule) to catch invalid names before the POST, and define a toast/inline-error component for server-returned 4xx/5xx responses from `/library/create`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `criterion_prompt_id` has ≥3 call sites justifying its own `ids.py`, or place it directly in `criteria/model.py` (the existing shared layer already imported by both the authoring path and descriptor-resolution path) to avoid a sub-Rule-of-Three standalone module.
findings.finding: `criterion_prompt_id` is proposed to live in a new standalone `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` but will have exactly 2 call sites (descriptor resolution in `registry._descriptor_from_prompt` and authoring in `editor._library_create`), which is below the Rule-of-Three threshold for a new module. The logic is a 2-branch one-liner that the existing `criteria/model.py` (already the shared layer imported by both sites) can trivially host without adding a new module to the import graph.
findings.location: Scope section / 'Add `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id) -> str` … in a new tiny `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `criterion_prompt_id` as a small public function in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/model.py` (already the shared layer). Import it from there in both `plan_review/registry.py` and `workflow/editor.py`. Delete the proposed `ids.py`. This satisfies the 'single source' requirement without a new module.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either update ADR 0015 to reflect that project criterion rubrics are now stored as `plan-review-project-<name>.md` (dashed, not dotted), or add a brief ADR/doc note for the `criterion_prompt_id` mapping contract in `ids.py`'s module docstring and reference it from ADR 0015.
findings.finding: The plan modifies the physical storage convention for project criterion rubrics (dotted filename → dashed filename), which is the contract governed by the referenced ADR 0015. The plan does not call for updating ADR 0015 or creating a new ADR for the `criterion_prompt_id` mapping pattern — leaving ADR 0015 potentially stale and the new mapping contract undocumented at the architectural level.
findings.location: Why section (references 'ef7e/ADR 0015'); Scope section ('No back-compat burden'); new `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` introduction

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for the editor UI flow in AC5: either a Playwright/Cypress/RTL test that renders the authoring form, enters a name, and asserts the displayed criterion id is `project.<name>` and the POST succeeds, OR add a documented rationale (e.g. 'UI is covered by manual smoke-test checklist at <link>') so the absence is deliberate rather than a gap.
findings.finding: The user-facing editor flow in AC5 ('a net-new author no longer dead-ends at a 400' and 'the editor authoring form surfaces the resulting criterion id') has no described proving command for the UI side and no documented rationale for its absence.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 (original section): 'The editor authoring form surfaces the resulting criterion id (project.<name>) so the user sees what will be created; a net-new author no longer dead-ends at a 400.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a11y done-definitions for the new criterion-id form field in `rebarProvider.jsx`: specify a visible `<label>` (or `aria-label`), keyboard focusability (Tab order), and sufficient color contrast per WCAG 2.1 AA SC 1.4.3, 4.1.2, and 2.1.1.
findings.finding: The plan adds a new interactive criterion-id form field to `rebarProvider.jsx` (an editor authoring form) but specifies no WCAG 2.1 AA done-definitions for this UI surface — no keyboard navigation, screen-reader label, contrast, or touch-target requirements are mentioned.
findings.location: 'Call-site responsibility (run 3)' section item 1; 'What' section (editor authoring form); AC5 (editor authoring form surfaces the resulting criterion id)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the RETIRE/cleanup path for a project criterion's prompt file + overlay pair (e.g., deletion removes both atomically), and clarify whether `create_prompt` is idempotent (overwrite) or errors on an existing file so retry behavior under partial failure is unambiguous.
findings.finding: No RETIRE/cleanup path is defined for the prompt file + overlay pair. The plan acknowledges a partial-failure case ('a failed overlay leaves an inactive, harmless prompt') but treats the stranded `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-<name>.md` as permanently harmless without specifying how it is ever removed. More broadly, no criterion deletion/retirement flow is described — if an operator removes the overlay entry but not the prompt file (or vice versa), state diverges silently.
findings.location: 'Call-site responsibility' section — step 2; Scope section ('no back-compat burden')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan (e.g., a git-revert note, an expand-contract sequence, or a one-line rollback script) for the fixture rewrites and call-site changes, so that a broken deployment can be recovered without a full re-investigation.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges breaking the overlay test fixtures and `author_criterion_overlay` API interface as an explicit justified choice, but provides NO rollback or back-out path. T4 requires the REMEDY to be an explicit rollback plan or expand-contract sequencing, not just acknowledgement. If the deployment is found broken (e.g., `criterion_prompt_id` mapping interacts unexpectedly with `enumerate_library` or the registry), there is no described mechanism to revert.
findings.location: Scope section: 'No back-compat burden (just-shipped feature)' + AC line: '(Back-compat is not a requirement — one clean path.)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under Scope: 'If post-deploy validation fails, revert via `git revert <sha>` — no data migration is needed because the ef7e fixtures are source-controlled and no external user data exists under the old dotted filenames.' This satisfies T4's remedy requirement with minimal overhead.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add observability (at minimum a structured log entry with criterion_id, prompt_id, and error detail) to the new failure points in `editor._library_create`: `create_prompt` failure and `write_criterion_overlay` failure.
findings.finding: The plan specifies write-ordering safety (prompt-first, then overlay) and atomic overlay rollback, but does not mention any observability (logging, metrics, or tracing) for the new failure points introduced in `editor._library_create` — specifically, failures of `create_prompt(prompt_id, ...)` or `write_criterion_overlay(...)` for project criteria.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the concurrency model for simultaneous criterion creation: state whether `create_prompt` is last-writer-wins (idempotent overwrite) or errors on conflict, and whether any guard (e.g., overlay existence pre-check or file lock) prevents a split-write outcome.
findings.finding: Concurrent authoring of the same `project.<name>` criterion by two simultaneous editor sessions is not addressed. The CREATE path is not atomic across the prompt-write and overlay-write steps, and neither step is described as guarded by a lock, CAS, or existence check. Two concurrent authors could both write the prompt file and then race on the overlay write.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider sequencing: land `criterion_prompt_id` + descriptor resolution + `/library/create` backend wiring first (testable without UI), then wire the editor form as a follow-on, so the riskiest integration is validated before the UI change.
findings.finding: The plan delivers a UI+backend change across 3 layers (editor UI, editor backend, criteria/descriptor backend) in a single leaf ticket with no thin vertical-slice or evidence-gated sequencing, making it a big-bang delivery where a mid-implementation breakage in any layer blocks the entire feature.
findings.location: Scope section and 'Call-site responsibility (remediation, run 3)' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the `production_batch_runner.py` prompt-id normalization path (lines 177–178) to the edit scope: after stripping `plan-review-`, detect the `project-` prefix and re-map to `project.` (e.g. `cid.replace('project-', 'project.', 1)` if `cid.startswith('project-')`) or look up via an inverse `criterion_prompt_id` helper, so `by_id()` lookup succeeds for sanitized project criterion prompt ids.
findings.finding: `production_batch_runner.py` lines 177–178 normalize a batch criterion's `prompt` field by stripping `_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX` (`plan-review-`). A workflow YAML authored after this change may carry `prompt: plan-review-project-foo` (the new sanitized form); stripping yields `project-foo`, which is absent from `registry.by_id()` (keyed by the dotted `project.foo`), causing the criterion to be silently skipped at run time. This file is listed in the plan's scope only for the `_PROJECT_PREFIX` import, not for a fix to this normalization path.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
